title: Rail ContextKit Adoption Plan
description: Example fixed-format ContextKit plan for adopting ContextKit in Rail docs.
humanSummary: Adopt ContextKit by converting a small set of canonical Rail docs first, then expand only after the template works for real runtime contracts.
agentSummary: Start from canonical specs and plans. Preserve Rail's source-of-truth hierarchy and add ContextKit validation before broad migration.
goal: Make Rail specs and plans easier for humans to scan while keeping them structured enough for AI coding agents to follow.
architecture: ContextKit stores document data as JSON and renders it through a fixed Astro web app. The JSON data remains the agent-readable contract.

in: Thin AGENTS.md linkage if adopted in Rail

nonGoals: Do not make free-form Markdown the document source.
Do not change Rail's product contract as part of template adoption.

surfaces.role: Generated fixed web docs app in target repos

currentState: Rail docs are plain Markdown with strong contracts but limited visual scanning support.
targetState: Rail docs use structured JSON as the contract and a fixed web page for human reading.

implementationSlices.doneWhen: Spec and plan pages build from JSON.

executionOrder: Run the generated docs app build.

risks.risk: JSON becomes verbose for humans to author.
risks.mitigation: Keep templates explicit and fixed; optimize rendering, not source looseness.

evidence: ContextKit templates and examples are validated by ./scripts/validate.sh.
agentInstructions: Treat this as an adoption plan shape, not a Rail migration command.
Do not edit Rail runtime code while adopting docs templates.
reviewChecklist: The plan distinguishes examples from active Rail authority.
Each implementation slice has a visible surface and done condition.
